formal_model_command_table.locus: lines 437-454, Counter value target ranges and command order
formal_model_command_table.as_interpretation: This is the canonical command-buffer map adopted by ADR-0026.

formal_model_execution_anchor.summary: The formal model sketches 32 target/message cases, clears the buffer, places self-target messages in a self mailbox, places neighbor-target messages on output channels, and then processes self special messages.
formal_model_execution_anchor.as_gap: Current AS Cell state has automail, control, buffer, input, and output, but no explicit self mailbox and no output-token model for all special messages.

legacy_divergences.locus: lines 206-222, buffer-process target and message cases
legacy_divergences.divergence: This differs from the formal command table, where 0-7 target self and offset 0 is standard-signal.
These legacy sketches define seven special messages and exclude standard-signal from that special-message list.

blockers.summary: The formal process-buffer path routes self-target commands through a self mailbox before special-message processing, but AS Cell does not yet model that mailbox.
The formal process-buffer path routes neighbor-target commands through output channels, but AS output channels currently model binary and stem-init tokens rather than all command messages.

ADR-0026 records standard-signal as command offset 0 from the formal command table, while legacy special-message lists omit it and at least one legacy sketch puts standard-signal at the final command case.

allowed_next_slices: Add an explicit self mailbox field and tests without executing every stem command.
Define an output-token representation for neighbor-target special messages without routing all commands.
Implement a narrow self-target execution subset only after the self mailbox and standard-signal boundary are documented.
Add more source-status tests if another PRC source resolves the target or command-order divergence.
